reference.title: Pushable Manifest Editor errors
reference.shortdesc: These errors can occur when you create a mapping for remote synchronization.

row.entry: The file is in an unsupported format. A non-path item was found when a path was expected. Open the .RemoteMapping file in a text editor and correct the line in error.
Open the Pushable Manifest Editor and set a valid remote resource type, or clear the mapping for that item.
Open the Pushable Manifest Editor and set a valid remote resource location.
Open the Pushable Manifest Editor and specify a remote resource type, or clear the remote location.
Open the Pushable Manifest Editor and specify a valid host location.
Open the Pushable Manifest Editor and specify a valid host location.

entry.msgph: The same remote resource is specified multiple times.

row.entry: Each remote resource can be specified only once per project. Open the Pushable Manifest Editor and set one of the local items in error to map to a different remote location.
Either create the local resource or clear the mapping from the editor.

section.p: Save the RemoteMapping file after you finish making any changes. If you have automatic build enabled, any errors display in the file appear in the Problems view. If automatic build is not enabled, start the build by choosing Project Build Project from the menu bar. If the Problems view is not visible, open it by selecting Window (on Windows) or (on macOS) > Show View Problems . Clicking any error in the Problems view highlights the line that must be corrected in the Pushable Manifest Editor.You must build the project for the Problems view to update.
